summary: Typosquat of tslib executes remote JavaScript fetched from an anonymous Vercel endpoint
details: The npm package `tsliverhome` impersonates Microsoft's `tslib` — its README is copied nearly verbatim from the legitimate `tslib` project (referencing `--importHelpers`, `__extends`/`__assign` helpers, and `github.com/microsoft/...`) while the package name is a lexical variant of `tslib`. The shipped code, however, implements none of the advertised TypeScript helper functions. Instead, `index.js` exports a function `buildoptimize` whose default parameters hardcode the URL `https://verceljs-kappa.vercel.app/icons/`; the function issues an HTTP GET to that endpoint and passes the response body to `eval(JSON.parse(b))`, with built-in retry logic. The destination is an anonymous Vercel subdomain unrelated to any legitimate publisher, the URL is not version-pinned, and the fetched payload is executed with no hash or signature verification. Any consumer that installs this package expecting `tslib`-like behavior and invokes the exported API will execute attacker-controlled JavaScript in the consumer's Node process. The cover-story naming (`setDefaultModule`, `buildoptimize`, `reqtoken`) and the README/code mismatch together indicate deliberate deception rather than a mistake.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
